tool_args.label: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (MTA)
tool_args.raw_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority ("MTA" or "MTA Group") was established under the New York Public Authorities Law and is a public benefit corporation and a component unit of the State of New York whose mission is to continue, develop, and improve public transportation and to develop and implement a unified public transportation policy in the New York metropolitan area.

payload.description: Public benefit corporation and component unit of the State of New York, established under the New York Public Authorities Law, providing transportation services in the New York metropolitan area, operations of seven bridges and two tunnels, and primary insurance coverage.

tool_args.label: MTA Audit Committee Meeting - October 2023
tool_args.raw_text: Audit Committee Meeting - Monday, 10/23/2023 12:00 - 1:00 PM ET

payload.description: MTA Audit Committee meeting held on October 23, 2023, 12:00-1:00 PM ET at MTA Board Room, 20th Floor 2 Broadway. Agenda included public comments, approval of minutes, work plan, quarterly financial statements, appointment of external auditors, audit approach, charter review, open audit recommendations, annual activity report, and executive session.

tool_args.label: No material weaknesses in 2022 Management Letter
tool_args.raw_text: There were no material weaknesses to report to the Committee

payload.claim_text: There were no material weaknesses to report to the Committee and Jill noted that all but one comments are low-level deficiencies
payload.subject: 2022 Management Letter for MTA and its agencies
payload.description: Deloitte reported no material weaknesses in the 2022 Management Letter. The one significant deficiency related to controls over non-operating revenue accounts and reconciliations.

evidence_refs.source_quote: There were no material weaknesses to report to the Committee and Jill noted that all but one comments are low-level deficiencies

tool_args.label: Ethics issues handled by MTA Corporate Compliance
tool_args.raw_text: they have handled over 2,600 ethics issues or inquiries

payload.claim_text: MTA Corporate Compliance has handled over 2,600 ethics issues or inquiries since the last report to the Audit Committee
payload.subject: MTA Corporate Compliance
payload.description: Lamond Kearse briefed the Committee on MTA Ethics Program. Key areas: 2,600+ ethics issues/inquiries handled, prohibitive source events via Ethics Request system, financial disclosure management, conflict of interest management with reverse two-year bar, 422 outside activity requests, and 26 ethics training sessions for over 2,000 employees.

evidence_refs.source_quote: handled over 2,600 ethics issues or inquiries

tool_args.label: PCAOB 2021 Inspection - Deloitte Part I.A deficiencies
tool_args.raw_text: Seven of the 54 audits we reviewed in 2021 are included in Part I.A of this report due to the significance of the deficiencies identified

payload.claim_text: Seven of the 54 audits reviewed in the 2021 PCAOB inspection of Deloitte & Touche LLP are included in Part I.A due to the significance of deficiencies identified. The identified deficiencies primarily related to the firm's testing of controls over and substantive testing of inventory.
payload.subject: Deloitte & Touche LLP 2021 PCAOB Inspection
payload.description: PCAOB Release No. 104-2022-219, November 4, 2022. 7 of 54 audits had Part I.A deficiencies. Most common deficiencies related to testing design/operating effectiveness of controls and testing controls over accuracy/completeness of data or reports.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Seven of the 54 audits we reviewed in 2021 are included in Part I.A of this report due to the significance of the deficiencies identified

tool_args.label: PCAOB 2021 Inspection - Audits with Part I.A deficiencies
tool_args.raw_text: Seven of the 54 audits we reviewed in 2021 are included in Part I.A of this report

payload.description: Seven of the 54 Deloitte & Touche LLP audits reviewed in the 2021 PCAOB inspection had Part I.A deficiencies

tool_args.label: Remediation Plans Implemented Awaiting Closure
tool_args.raw_text: MTA Corporate Compliance Remediation Plans Monitoring Six Months Past Due - Report to the Audit Committee October 2023

payload.claim_text: Remediation plans implemented awaiting closure by agency and priority: High priority - MTA Headquarters (18), Construction & Development Company (2), Metro-North Railroad (2), Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority (2). Medium priority - MTA Headquarters (3), MTA Bus Company (3); MTA Headquarters (1), MTA Bus Company (3); MTA Headquarters (3), MTA Bus Company (3). Low priority - MTA Headquarters (38), New York City Transit Authority (8), New York City Transit Authority (6).
